name: Tanos, Santiurde de Toranzo, Puente Viesgo, Alto de La Montaña – circular route
name_original: Tanos, Santiurde de Toranzo, Puente Viesgo, Alto de La Montaña – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling around Torrelavega offers diverse landscapes, from large flat valleys to challenging mountain passes. The region's strategic location provides direct access to bike paths connecting the coast with the interior, making it suitable for various skill levels. Cyclists can experience rolling coastal hills that gradually ascend into the Cantabrian Mountains, with two converging rivers forming green rings around urban areas. This varied terrain, including popular road bike trails, supports both leisurely rides and significant ascents. Best road cycling routes around Torrelavega The most popular road cycling route is Tanos, Santiurde de Toranzo, Puente Viesgo, Alto de La Montaña – circular route, a 39.8 miles (64.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 17 minutes to complete. This route features varied terrain including an ascent to Alto de La Montaña. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Collada de Carmona – Stone Fountain loop from Torrelavega-Centro, a moderate 49.7 miles (80.1 km) path. This route includes the Collada de Carmona, offering a notable climb and scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Section between sea and mountains – Views of the Zapedo marsh loop from Torrelavega, a 44.7 miles (71.9 km) trail leading through coastal and mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 32 minutes. Road cycling around Torrelavega is defined by coastal hills, river valleys, and ascents into the Cantabrian Mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flat sections to challenging climbs. The routes in Torrelavega are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 2800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Torrelavega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Torrelavega?
A: Torrelavega offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with nearly 50 different tours available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from leisurely rides to challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Torrelavega?
A: The region around Torrelavega boasts incredibly diverse terrain. You'll find large flat valleys, rolling coastal hills that gradually ascend into the Cantabrian Mountains, and direct access to bike paths connecting the coast with the interior. This variety allows for both relaxed rides and significant climbs.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Torrelavega?
A: Yes, Torrelavega has several road cycling routes perfect for beginners. There are 8 easy routes available, often utilizing bike paths and flatter sections, providing a great way to explore the area at a comfortable pace.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Torrelavega offers 9 difficult routes. These often include significant ascents into the Cantabrian Mountains, such as the Collada de Carmona, and routes with considerable elevation gain like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005115667/tanos-santiurde-de-toranzo-puente-viesgo-alto-de-la-montana-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tanos, Santiurde de Toranzo, Puente Viesgo, Alto de La Montaña – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which features an ascent to Alto de La Montaña.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes near Torrelavega?
A: Road cycling routes around Torrelavega often pass by stunning natural features and landmarks. You can encounter spectacular beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Justa Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3688018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Sable Beach&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views from peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4370882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Pico La Capía (Pico Dobra)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3597279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Montaña&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its proximity to World Heritage-classified caves and charming medieval towns like Santillana del Mar.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer breathtaking scenic viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20169903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada de Carmona – Stone Fountain loop from Torrelavega-Centro&lt;/a&gt; provides notable climbs with expansive views. You can also find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1511383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Ballota&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3597279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Montaña&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the diverse Cantabrian landscape.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Torrelavega?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which allows for everything from coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents, and the strategic location that provides access to both natural beauty and cultural sites.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Torrelavega?
A: Yes, Torrelavega is well-suited for family cycling. The region offers direct access to bike paths that connect the coast with the interior, providing safe and enjoyable options for families. These routes often feature flatter sections and less traffic, making them ideal for all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Torrelavega?
A: Torrelavega is an excellent destination for road cycling throughout much of the year. The diverse landscapes mean you can find suitable routes in different seasons. While specific weather conditions vary, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, though summer can also be great for coastal rides.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Torrelavega are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005115667/tanos-santiurde-de-toranzo-puente-viesgo-alto-de-la-montana-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tanos, Santiurde de Toranzo, Puente Viesgo, Alto de La Montaña – circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31546374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Section between sea and mountains – Views of the Zapedo marsh loop from Torrelavega&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that combine coastal views with inland exploration?
A: Absolutely. Torrelavega's unique location, just six kilometers from the coast and with easy access to the Cantabrian Mountains, means many routes offer a fantastic blend of coastal scenery and inland exploration. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31546374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Section between sea and mountains – Views of the Zapedo marsh loop from Torrelavega&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading through both coastal and mountainous landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that follow river valleys?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by two converging rivers that form green rings around its urban areas, offering pleasant routes along river valleys. The Saja River Cycle Path is a notable option, providing a moderate ride through scenic natural surroundings.
tips: The pre-Roman stelae of Barros and Lombera stand out, as well as the manor houses that can be found in the Rasilla park area, such as the palace of the Counts of Mansilla, Bustamante and Quijano. One of the main attractions are the Cantabrian Wars festivities, which are held on the last weekend of August and the first of September, recreating the wars between the Romans and the Cantabrians.

This church is located in Vejorís de Toranzo, located on a hill where it stands out from the center of the town.
Its construction is from the 16th century, its Renaissance portal with three naves and fluted shaft columns. Inside, there is a Renaissance main altarpiece, with three sections and carved panels with the Annunciation, Visitation and Three Wise Men, and this is where the exceptional enamel altarpiece comes from, a jewel that is currently preserved in the Santillana Diocesan Museum.

Also known as El Puente Rual, the aqueduct is next to the Vía Verde del Pas. It was built in 1885 in order to supply drinking water to the city of Santander. It is an ideal place to take a short break – there is shade, benches and tables. In front there is a bar and a fountain.

German-made locomotive that entered service in 1913 to transport iron ores and limestone from the Camargo mines to its factory. It is located on the greenway, near the train station, converted into an exhibition hall and tourist information office.
The Mountain through Las Presillas has a length of 3.16km, an altitude of 195 meters and an average slope of 4.5%. From here you can admire the beautiful hills and the great views.
